# Load the text
path = "/mnt/data/file-Y18ScoRwDeEahM8yCBCTnu-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    raw = f.read()
raw

import re
raw = raw
# Find the spans inside the initial quotes
print(raw)

# Extract substrings within double quotes
subs = []
start = 0
while True:
    a = raw.find('"', start)
    if a == -1: break
    b = raw.find('"', a+1)
    if b == -1: break
    subs.append((a, b, raw[a+1:b]))
    start = b+1

subs

# Find all occurrences of the substring starting with the specific hamza phrase inside the raw
targets = []
for m in re.finditer("ءَأَنتَ", raw):
    targets.append(m.start())
targets[:10], len(targets)

# Extract all parentheses substrings that contain the hamza phrase
paren_spans = []
start = 0
while True:
    a = raw.find('(', start)
    if a == -1: break
    b = raw.find(')', a+1)
    if b == -1: break
    content = raw[a+1:b]
    if "ءَأَنتَ" in content or "وَإِذۡ قَالَ" in content:
        paren_spans.append((a, b, content))
    start = b+1

paren_spans

# Find the exact "لَقَدْ كَفَرَ" phrase boundaries
m = re.search("لَقَدْ كَفَرَ[^\[]+", raw)  # up to before bracket
m.group(0)[:120], m.start(), m.end()

spans = []

# From quotes (first three)
quote_spans = subs[:3]
for a,b,content in quote_spans:
    start = a+1
    end = b
    # assert
    assert raw[start:end] == content
    spans.append((start, end, content, "Aya"))

# From parentheses
for a,b,content in paren_spans:
    text = content
    # trim trailing punctuation like ... and any punctuation at edges
    trimmed = text.strip(' \n\r\t\u200f\u200e"«»()[]{}<>،؛:.,!؟…ـ')
    # but we must only trim edges; our stripping handled both sides; now find where this trimmed is within the original content
    # Compute left offset
    left_offset = text.find(trimmed)
    start = a+1+left_offset
    end = start + len(trimmed)
    assert raw[start:end] == trimmed
    spans.append((start, end, trimmed, "Aya"))

# Add the "لَقَدْ كَفَرَ ..." ayah
m = re.search("لَقَدْ كَفَرَ[^\[]+", raw)
aya2_text = raw[m.start():m.end()].rstrip(' \n\r\t')
# maybe trailing punctuation comma is after bracket; okay
start = raw.find(aya2_text, m.start()-1, m.end()+1)
end = start + len(aya2_text)
assert raw[start:end] == aya2_text
spans.append((start, end, aya2_text, "Aya"))

len(spans), spans[:2]
